name: Cerveny Kapusta
recipeInstructions:
1. Render pork fat very slowly and remove the cracklings.
2. Add enough finely chopped onion for taste and flavor, and cook until golden.
3. Gradually add paprika to the mixture without letting it burn or smoke.
4. Let cool and store in a covered container in the refrigerator.
5. Wash the cabbage in cold water, quarter it, and chop finely while salting as you go. Discard the core.
6. Heat about 3 cups of the pork-fat/onion mixture. Add about 1/2 teaspoon paprika without letting it burn.
7. Add the salty cabbage and stir until it is coated with fat.
8. Add pepper to taste, grated apple, and stir in.
9. Add about 2-3 tablespoons white vinegar, 1 tablespoon sugar, and rub about 1 1/2 teaspoons caraway seeds between your palms before adding them to the cabbage.
This traditional recipe will yield different results each time until you achieve the desired flavor. The best taste is often found when reheating leftovers on the second or third day.
